mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates weakening economic activity and reduced corporate profitability, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate slower growth and potential recession.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed a decline in August 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where PAYEMS shows a sustained decline over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a critical threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or coincides with economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence erodes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 6.1% in Aug 2008, up from 4.6% in Aug 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at cooling an overheating economy, which typically dampens corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY was high in mid-2008
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive or flat in periods where both conditions are met, indicating no dampening effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recession, a sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which further exacerbates the downturn and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue expectations fall.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS showed a decline in August 2008
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances during declared recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced output and efficiency.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO showed a decline in August 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of periods where INDPRO is declining over 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level indicates a severe downturn in the housing market, which has broad negative implications for the economy and S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced construction and related spending.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of periods when HOUST is below 1000, indicating resilience despite housing weakness.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) is rising rapidly while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains elevated, it suggests a policy error or an inability of monetary policy to address the economic slowdown, leading to prolonged negative sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.3pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 6.1% in Aug 2008, FEDFUNDS was 2.0% in Aug 2008
regime_hint: economic slowdown with sticky monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both conditions are met, suggesting policy is not a drag.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals persistent inflationary pressures, which erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as real earnings decline.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 219.181 in Jul 2008, indicating high inflation
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances during high inflation regimes, indicating equities are a hedge.
